purpose: Resolve or explicitly scope the eight unresolved items left open by PR #126 so a future, separate implementation issue can be opened without guessing. Implementation-design only; does not authorize production binding.
decision_rationale: Every one of the eight unresolved items from PR #126 section 5 is addressed: resolved with a concrete default, given an explicit decision rule, specified as a checklist, scoped as a requirement without being implemented, or represented as a concrete governance mechanism. Nothing is left open-ended for a future implementation issue to guess at.

unresolved_item_resolutions.decision: Do not lock source_dataset_refs.artifact_sha256 here. Reference the current known identity (path exports/promoted/nfl/player_season_coverage_v0.json, sha256 29f8e378127fa5426e5897ac4522b6187941312edabab357d8a427fb20511035, promotion review TIBER-Data#192/PR#193) as informative context only. The implementation issue must re-verify this identity via npm run gate:player-history-promoted-source and npm run refresh:player-history-promoted-mirrors before locking any value.

production_only is the v0 default per the #116 attribution (joined MAE 40.173 vs full set 40.034, a 0.138 gap). Expanding to the full feature set requires clearing an added-value test (improvement must exceed the eventual acceptance-threshold bar, not just be nonzero) and a governance test (no expanded forbidden-field surface; reviewed as its own contract amendment, minimum MINOR version bump).

N = 3 prior seasons is the v0 default, matching the #112/#122 experiments. Changing N requires re-running the full LOOCV/train-fold-only/shuffled-control experimental design against the new window, not a config edit.

No number set here. A future threshold proposal must: preserve real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled framing; evaluate on the production split semantics active at proposal time; state both a relative-improvement floor and an absolute joined-MAE floor; explicitly acknowledge single-season risk (current evidence is one replicated season); and carry the human sign-off from item 8.

Required checklist: target-season exclusion, partial-season exclusion, generated_at timing vs actual inference-time availability, real inference-time field availability, documented train/eval split semantics, source-artifact freshness/staleness policy, derived-feature look-ahead risk, missing-history treatment. Drafting the checklist into a document is future implementation-issue work; running it against a real proposal is wiring-issue-time work.

Must take a locked source_dataset_refs + contract_version as input; produce a conforming contract instance with a recomputable run_id as output; fail closed on source-identity mismatch, forbidden fields, and null-semantics violations; and reproduce the committed #122 joined MAE/RMSE (baseline 68.926, real 40.034, shuffled 72.031; RMSE 88.553/57.287/90.409) as a smoke test when pointed at the same identity. Not implemented in this document; building it is in-scope for the future implementation issue (see future_implementation_issue_boundary.allowed).

Any future validation/replay output must report the no-history subgroup count, share, and by-position breakdown, plus confirmation every no-history player's feature block is entirely null. Silent pass-through with no reporting is non-conforming even if null-handling is technically correct.

Sign-off must be a comment/review on the specific wiring PR (not a blanket pre-approval), must reference the exact run_id/contract_version, confirm the leakage-audit checklist passed, cite the acceptance-threshold evidence relied on, be dated, and be attributable to the repository's merge-authority operator account. No external or unavailable approver is named.

non_negotiables_preserved: target season S may only use seasons < S -- no exceptions
no target-season or partial-target-season substitution, under any feature-availability fallback
missing-history players must not be silently zero-filled -- entire feature block stays null, and its presence must be reported
source artifact identity must be path + sha256 + promotion review, never sha256 alone; mismatch fails closed
run_id must derive from the full source identity plus contract_version, generator_script_version, and generated_at
the real-vs-baseline-vs-shuffled validation framing remains required for any acceptance-threshold proposal
this document does not authorize seasonalPprModel.ts wiring, in whole or in part
any Fantasy/product consumer requires its own separate, explicitly-approved review

allowed: create a non-production contract schema/type or JSON Schema representing the amended v0 shape
create validation logic checking a contract instance against that schema (structural validation only, not a production consumer)
create the deterministic replay/validation script specified in unresolved_item_resolutions.deterministic_implementation_replay_command
lock source_dataset_refs for a specific implementation artifact, following the re-verification requirement in unresolved_item_resolutions.exact_artifact_sha_lock
generate an experimental/non-production contract instance under an experiment-scoped path
add tests for schema conformance, provenance/run_id recomputation, fail-closed identity-mismatch behavior, and missing-history null-and-reporting behavior
still_forbidden_unless_separately_approv: seasonalPprModel.ts wiring, in whole or in part
production model feature use of any kind

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: choosing the full feature scope just because it has a slightly lower MAE
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: feature_family_scope resolution requires clearing an added-value test against a not-yet-set threshold bar

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: locking a stale artifact sha too early
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: exact_artifact_sha_lock resolution requires re-verification via existing fail-closed gate scripts at implementation time

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: treating N=3 as permanent without review
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: rolling_window_length resolution requires re-running the full experimental design for any change

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: setting a production threshold based on one replicated season only
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: production_acceptance_threshold resolution requires explicit single-season-risk acknowledgment

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: implementing replay that does not verify source identity
failure_modes_prevented.prevention: deterministic_implementation_replay_command resolution requires fail-closed source-identity verification as required behavior
missing_history_subgroup_reporting resolution requires visible subgroup reporting, never zero-fill

failure_modes_prevented.failure_mode: letting contract implementation become model wiring by accident

non_goals: does not implement any part of the contract in code
does not create a TypeScript runtime schema or validation logic (future implementation-issue work)
does not lock source_dataset_refs.artifact_sha256 to a concrete value
does not resolve feature-family scope with a final answer beyond the default rule (a future amendment may still justify the full set)
does not set a production acceptance threshold or number
does not run the leakage-audit checklist against any real proposal, only specifies it
does not approve a Fantasy consumer, UI surface, ranking, or advice behavior
does not promote or demote any TIBER-Data artifact
does not modify seasonalPprModel.ts or any other production Forecast file
next_step: The next_step_decision permits opening a SEPARATE implementation issue scoped exactly to future_implementation_issue_boundary.allowed. That issue must respect every non_negotiables_preserved entry and every gate established by PR #124 and PR #126, and must not attempt seasonalPprModel.ts wiring, production feature generation consumed by production code, or any Fantasy/product consumer change.
